meaning: adj.自由的,无拘束的,未受控制的
examples: The US space station Skylab came back partially uncontrolled in 1979. Parts of the station fell in western Australia, but no one was hurt.
The thoughts rushed into my mind uncontrolled.
His uncontrolled behaviour disturbed the entire class.
But uncontrolled growth without continual pruning and streamlining is a potential recipe for disaster.
ERS-1 and Envisat both suffered major failures that left them drifting uncontrolled through LEO.
